items.title: The Eye of Osiris, by R. Austin Freeman
items.content: <article>A newly-qualified doctor helps his former teacher, a famed forensic scientist, unravel a case involving ancient Egyptian artifacts, a missing person, and a mysterious will.</article>

items.title: The Innocents Abroad, by Mark Twain
items.content: <article>Mark Twain travels abroad, offering witty observations on the places and cultures he encounters and commentary on the habits and foibles of his fellow travelers.</article>

items.title: Unspoken Sermons, by George MacDonald
items.content: <article>George MacDonald presents a set of interlocking sermons designed to shape the perception and life of the Christian disciple.</article>

items.title: The Tour, by Louis Couperus
items.content: <article>A Roman nobleman travels through Egypt in search of a lost love.</article>

items.title: The Nebuly Coat, by John Meade Falkner
items.content: <article>While overseeing renovations of a medieval church, a young architect is drawn unwillingly into local mysteries.</article>

items.title: Manhattan Transfer, by John Dos Passos
items.content: <article>Several characters strive for happiness and excitement in early twentieth century New York City.</article>

items.title: William—An Englishman, by Cicely Hamilton
items.content: <article>A young activist couple’s lives turn for the worse when the unexpected arrival of foreign soldiers interrupts their honeymoon.</article>

items.title: The Trachiniae, by Sophocles
items.content: <article>A woman unintentionally kills her demigod husband with a poisoned robe in a desperate attempt to reclaim his love.</article>

items.title: Memoirs of a Foxhunting Man, by Siegfried Sassoon
items.content: <article>A young boy comes of age in the idyllic English countryside just before the Great War tears Europe apart.</article>

items.title: The Giant Raft, by Jules Verne
items.content: <article>An ex-slave trader with an encrypted note schemes about its meaning, while a man with a secret past travels with his family down the Amazon in a giant raft to attend his daughter’s wedding.</article>
